candidates.why: Explicit COGS-related D&A, but it appears to be a component within the selected COGS bucket ('Cost of revenues/COGS' / 'Cost of Revenues') rather than a separate expense to add on top; adding it would risk double counting.
This appears to be a broader operating D&A line and not specifically tied to the selected COGS bucket; the statement already has a dedicated COGS D&A row, so this should not be mapped to COGS_DA.

variables.notes: Selected COGS rows appear to be parent/total cost buckets that already include the explicit 'Depreciation/Amortization in COR/COGS' component.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above operating profit and outside the selected XSGA component rows. Since COGS has its own explicit D&A row, this broader D&A line most plausibly represents non-COGS operating D&A that should be added separately to XSGA.
Explicitly belongs to COR/COGS, not SG&A/overhead, so it should not be mapped to XSGA_DA.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket consists of component rows (Other Operating Expenses, Administrative Expenses, Selling and Marketing Expenses, Personnel Expenses) and does not appear to already include this separate same-level D&A line.
